introduction.p: Polynomial, rational, and algebraic functions are vaguely familiar systems. We also must expand to include exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric functions.

section.title: Polynomials
section.p: A polynomial (in a ring R) is a finitely supported sequence in R with addition given component-wise and multiplication given by convolution (with respect to (\mathbb{N},+))

statement.p: Write a computer program that will implement the Euclidean algorithm. The program should accept two positive integers a and b as input and should output \gcd( a,b) as well as integers r and s such that \gcd( a,b) = ra + sb.
